content:
Task task-041 completed: Configured the production nginx reverse proxy and committed it as `a159abe` (`[task-041] Configure nginx reverse proxy`). Updated `nginx/nginx.conf` to disable `server_tokens`, keep the required `/health` endpoint, apply SPA fallback, serve `index.html` and `metadata.json` with no-cache headers, and cache hashed Expo assets under `/_expo/static/` for one year with immutable cache-control. Added `nginx/security-headers.conf` with the requested security headers plus a CSP that allows the Supabase domains and Google OAuth endpoints, and added `nginx/ssl.conf` as a dormant HTTPS server template for the follow-on SSL task. Updated `Dockerfile` to copy the full `nginx/` directory into `/etc/nginx/` so the new include files exist inside the runtime image.

Verification: `SUPABASE_URL=https://example.supabase.co SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=public-anon-key npm run build:web` passed; `npm run typecheck` passed. `nginx -t` could not be run in this environment because the `nginx` binary is unavailable. `git pull --rebase` was attempted first and failed because the shared worktree contained unrelated unstaged changes; the task files were then committed directly without touching those unrelated changes.

Definition of Done notes: input validation, authentication, authorization, and sanitized client error handling were not applicable because this task only adds static web-server configuration, not app endpoints. No dedicated automated happy-path/error-path test harness exists for nginx config in this repo yet; verification was limited to web export, typecheck, manual config inspection, and the commit.

`task-041` is complete. Runtime contract stayed on port 80 with `GET /health` returning 200 OK, so your compose healthcheck can stay aligned to `http://localhost:80/health` (or container-local `:80/health`). Commit: a159abe.

Heads-up from `task-042`: the SSL template expects `/etc/nginx/certs/{fullchain.pem,privkey.pem,dhparam.pem}` plus an ACME webroot at `/var/www/certbot`, and it keeps plain HTTP `/health` alive while redirecting other HTTP traffic. If your compose task is still open and you want it SSL-ready, the useful optional additions are `443:443`, `./certs:/etc/nginx/certs:ro`, and `/var/www/certbot:/var/www/certbot:ro`.

Task task-042 completed: Created SSL certificate setup assets and committed them as `d948afb` (`[task-042] Create SSL certificate setup`). Updated `nginx/ssl.conf` into a full HTTPS replacement template with an HTTP redirect server, ACME challenge exception at `/.well-known/acme-challenge/`, preserved plain HTTP `/health`, and TLS 1.2/1.3 settings with a Mozilla-compatible intermediate cipher list plus DH/session tuning. Added `scripts/setup-ssl.sh` for the Let's Encrypt webroot flow (Certbot install, `certonly --webroot`, cron-based renew with deploy hook), `scripts/setup-ssl-selfsigned.sh` for local/dev certificates, and `scripts/sync-letsencrypt-certs.sh` to normalize runtime cert files into `./certs`. Added `docs/SSL_SETUP.md` documenting both flows, Docker mount requirements, and how to promote `nginx/ssl.conf`, and updated `.gitignore` to exclude `certs/` so generated private keys are not committed.

Verification: `bash -n scripts/setup-ssl.sh scripts/setup-ssl-selfsigned.sh scripts/sync-letsencrypt-certs.sh` passed; `npm run typecheck` passed; `SUPABASE_URL=https://example.supabase.co SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=public-anon-key npm run build:web` passed. `nginx -t` could not be run in this environment because the `nginx` binary is unavailable. `git pull --rebase` was attempted first and failed because the shared worktree contained unrelated unstaged changes; the SSL files were then committed directly without touching those unrelated changes.

Definition of Done notes: input validation, authentication, authorization, and sanitized client error handling were not applicable because this task only adds deployment config, shell automation, and docs. No dedicated automated happy-path/error-path test harness exists yet for these SSL scripts or nginx templates, so verification was limited to shell parsing, web export, typecheck, manual config inspection, and the commit.

Task task-013 completed: Implemented the shared auth state layer and committed it as `4fdf2d3` (`[task-013] Implement auth context and hooks`). Added `src/contexts/AuthContext.tsx` with `AuthProvider`, session restoration via `supabase.auth.getSession()`, live updates via `supabase.auth.onAuthStateChange()`, Google OAuth sign-in using PKCE redirect targets, sign-out, and profile fetching from `public.users` with `UserSchema` validation before storing it in context. Added `src/hooks/useAuth.ts` and `src/hooks/useSession.ts`, exported them through the existing barrels, and updated `App.tsx` to wrap the app with `ThemeProvider` and `AuthProvider` so the new context is active in the running app.

Verification: `npm run typecheck` passed; `npm run lint -- App.tsx src/contexts/AuthContext.tsx src/contexts/index.ts src/hooks/useAuth.ts src/hooks/useSession.ts src/hooks/index.ts` passed (aside from ESLint’s expected eslintrc deprecation warning from the repo tooling bridge); `SUPABASE_URL=https://example.supabase.co SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=public-anon-key npm run build:web` passed. `git pull --rebase` was attempted first and failed because the shared worktree contained unrelated unstaged changes; the auth files were then committed directly without touching those unrelated changes.

Definition of Done notes: external data loaded from `public.users` is validated with `UserSchema`; authentication is handled by restoring and tracking the Supabase session; authorization for profile reads continues to rely on the existing Supabase session plus RLS policies. This task does not add a server endpoint, so request-param validation and server-side error status handling were not applicable. No dedicated automated happy-path/error-path auth test harness exists in the repo yet, so validation was limited to lint, typecheck, web export, manual inspection, and the commit.

Task task-043 completed: Created the Raspberry Pi deployment guide and automation artifacts, committed as `c8f5af3` (`[task-043] Add Raspberry Pi deployment guide`). Added `docs/RASPBERRY_PI_DEPLOYMENT.md` covering Raspberry Pi OS 64-bit flashing, first-boot setup, Docker installation via `get.docker.com` with an official-doc caveat, Compose plugin verification, UFW configuration, repository cloning, `.env` setup, production container startup, optional HTTPS integration using the SSL task outputs, systemd auto-start, performance tuning, backup guidance, and troubleshooting. Added `scripts/deploy.sh` to enforce a clean git worktree, `git pull --ff-only`, rebuild the production compose stack, and restart it using either `docker compose` or `docker-compose`. Added `scripts/systemd/worktracker.service` as a boot-time `Type=oneshot` unit that reruns the deploy script against `docker-compose.prod.yml`.

Verification: `bash -n scripts/deploy.sh` passed; `git diff --check -- docs/RASPBERRY_PI_DEPLOYMENT.md scripts/deploy.sh scripts/systemd/worktracker.service` passed; `npm run typecheck` passed. `git pull --rebase` was attempted first and failed because the shared worktree contained unrelated unstaged changes; the Pi deployment files were then committed directly without touching those unrelated changes.

Definition of Done notes: input validation, authentication, authorization, and sanitized client error handling were not applicable because this task only adds deployment documentation and shell/systemd automation. No dedicated automated happy-path/error-path test harness exists for the deployment script or systemd unit in this repo, so verification was limited to shell parsing, typecheck, diff checks, and the commit.

Task task-014 calls for a Google icon from `@expo/vector-icons`, but that package is not installed in this repo (`require.resolve('@expo/vector-icons')` fails) and network/package installation is not available in this environment. I’m proceeding with a no-dependency fallback: a locally rendered Google-style badge inside the button, while keeping the rest of the login screen/auth flow aligned with the task and the existing auth context.

Task task-014 completed: Implemented the Google OAuth login screen flow in the Expo app and committed it as `a8b6fc5` (`[task-014] Add login screen with Google auth`). Added `LoginScreen`, `GoogleSignInButton`, and auth barrel exports; wired `App.tsx` to show the login screen when unauthenticated; and updated the shared `Button` component to support non-text children so the auth button can render custom content safely. Used a local Google badge fallback instead of `@expo/vector-icons` because that package is not present in this repo and dependency installs are restricted; this was escalated and the screen remains functional without adding dependencies. Validation/tests completed: `npm run typecheck`, targeted `npm run lint -- App.tsx src/components/ui/Button.tsx src/components/index.ts src/components/auth/GoogleSignInButton.tsx src/components/auth/index.ts src/screens/LoginScreen.tsx src/screens/index.ts`, and `SUPABASE_URL=https://example.supabase.co SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=public-anon-key npm run build:web` all passed. Input validation/auth/authorization/error-handling checklist items are not directly applicable because this task adds UI only, but error handling was included for sign-in initiation failures with inline and platform alert feedback. `git pull --rebase` was attempted before commit but blocked by unrelated unstaged changes elsewhere in the shared worktree, so the commit was made as a path-limited isolated change.

Task task-012 completed: Implemented the development seed workflow and committed it as `cefb4bc` (`[task-012] Add development seed workflow`). Added `scripts/seed-dev.ts`, a no-dependency TypeScript runner executed via Node `--experimental-strip-types`; it uses `SUPABASE_URL` plus `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY`, creates or reuses two deterministic dev auth users, upserts matching `public.users` profiles, and upserts removable uncategorized sample `time_entries` plus overall `monthly_goals` using fixed IDs for idempotency. Added `supabase/seed.sql` as a local SQL fallback that only seeds sample entries/goals for pre-existing matching users and intentionally does not create categories. Added `docs/DEVELOPMENT.md` documenting the workflow/flags and updated `.env.example` plus `package.json` with the new env var and `seed:dev` script. Validation completed: `npm run typecheck` passed, targeted `npm run lint -- scripts/seed-dev.ts` passed aside from the repo’s existing ESLintRC deprecation warning, and `npm run seed:dev -- --dry-run` succeeded; Node prints a non-fatal `MODULE_TYPELESS_PACKAGE_JSON` warning because the repo is not marked as an ES module package, but the script executes correctly. Input validation/auth/authorization checklist items are limited here because this task adds internal tooling rather than a client-facing endpoint; the script validates required env vars, never hardcodes secrets, and keeps the service role key server-side only. There is no existing `npm test` script in this package, so no unit/integration test suite could be run for this task. `git pull --rebase` was attempted before commit but blocked by unrelated unstaged changes elsewhere in the shared worktree, so the commit was made as a path-limited isolated change.
Received wind_down. Finished task-012, committed it as cefb4bc ([task-012] Add development seed workflow), marked it complete, and am pausing now without claiming additional tasks.
